findings.justification: The currency symbol returned by get_woocommerce_currency_symbol() is concatenated directly into the HTML span without escaping, so any filter that injects HTML into the symbol string will produce stored XSS in price output.

When $regular_price or $sale_price is non-numeric the raw value is placed verbatim inside <del> and <ins> tags with no escaping, allowing a caller or filter that controls those arguments to inject arbitrary HTML.

wp_kses_post() is used to sanitize the price decimal and thousand separator options, which permits HTML tags; these separator strings are later interpolated into rendered price HTML without further escaping, enabling stored XSS by any admin who can save WooCommerce settings.

html_entity_decode() is called on the raw coupon value before sanitize_post_field() and wp_kses(), which can re-introduce decoded HTML that was stored as entities, potentially bypassing sanitization for coupon codes that contain HTML entity-encoded payloads.
